subject: I'm so proud of Tim

body:
| | Derek, I am so proud of the work Tim has done.

But his opponent in this race has spent this entire summer trying to convince people that Tim hasn't accomplished anything for Virginia. **That 's just flat-out wrong.**

For as long as I've known him, Tim has been dedicated to public service. It's one of the reasons I first fell in love with him. And I've watched him work so hard year after year to improve the lives of Virginians.

name: Melania Trump
subject: I never envisioned
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican National Committee
body:
From the moment my husband, Donald, placed his hand on the bible |
---
| | | | | | |
Friend,

>From the moment my husband, Donald, placed his hand on the bible and took the oath of office to serve as America's President, he has worked nonstop to deliver on his promises.

Democrats and the opposition media are doing everything they possibly can to discredit Donald with false accusations by spreading their fake news and making it appear that he does not have the support of America's voters.

**[That is why I am asking that you join my husband as he fights for our nation by signing your Presidential Pledge of Support.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=0e0286f2dfd17249f7701048f9e1b419452a600cfaafce45ac4a7b05b8a6f5f331cdaa8534d364cf8d0bc141636ea335f245310cbb4936ba)**

Up until a few years ago, I never envisioned that I would one day serve our nation as First Lady.

Everything you and I believe in regarding the future of America is on the line in the coming months. _This is a battle we must win together._

I'm asking you and other Americans across the country to personally register your support and help prove that the Democrats and media are wrong.

**[Please sign your Presidential Pledge of Support. I look forward to telling Donald that you are standing with him.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=0e0286f2dfd172492009a2bda33ffdc13ed28b739d0bf4c3a4e9fbdf77b9a9f76ab2a850136bc504178faad7f55fa8473963cf24e79ee8f6)**

Sincerely,

Melania Trump

subject: We're making progress despite the chaos
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE
body:
|
---

| | | This email has a lot of information about what I've been working on, and I hope you will take a moment to read it.

**_[I need your support for my re-election campaign, and I want to make sure you understand why and how I'm working to earn it.](http://nm.martinheinrich. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mth-sunday-deadline-alert?amount=29&recurring=true&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=1+-+I+need+your+support+for+my+reelection+ca&utm_campaign=MH_EM_FR_90DO_180826_1_1&source=MH_EM_FR_90DO_180826_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_FR_90DO_180826_1_1")_**

* * *

Nearly every day, we face news about President Trump's scandals, his lies, and the political catastrophes created by his administration. But that shouldn't detract from the progress that is being made despite his chaos.

**I have been leading the conversation on cybersecurity and the threats we face from foreign hackers.** Beyond our election infrastructure, our energy grids and transportation systems are also susceptible to hacking attacks, with potentially disastrous consequences. We secured $380 million for election security earlier this year, and my amendment to establish a national cyber warfare doctrine passed the Senate earlier this month.

**I 've also been fighting to protect investments in our rural transportation infrastructure in New Mexico.** The Senate recently passed my bipartisan amendment for an additional $50 million to help maintain the Southwest Chief Amtrak route. Trains help connect New Mexico to the rest of the country, and keeping this route is critical to the local economy and the rural communities that rely on this line.

**And I 'm standing up to President Trump's cynical attempt to force consumers to bailout uncompetitive coal and nuclear power plants. **In a blatant effort to keep his campaign promises, the president is claiming a national security emergency to keep coal-fired plants open. But the truth is there is no national security emergency, and this artificial bailout will increase energy costs and leave businesses and households paying higher electric bills. I am going to keep fighting against this bailout and any attempt to drag us back on energy, and I will continue to push for modernizing and diversifying our energy grid.

Some of these updates don't warrant a breaking news alert on your phone, but they are making a difference in people's lives. I came to the Senate to represent New Mexicans and our values, and we are making progress that will help people across our state and country.

**_[That is why I am asking for your support today. I am in my first Senate re-election race, and all this hard work and more will be undone if one of my extreme right-wing opponents defeats me this November. To make sure that doesn't happen, we need to reach our weekend fundraising goal by bringing in $5,000 in grassroots donations. Will you pitch in and help me reach this goal before the midnight deadline?](http://nm.martinheinrich. name: Gina Raimondo
subject: how far we've come
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Gina Raimondo
body:
Friend, if you're like me this time of year, you're trying to figure out how to fit in a couple more days at Sand Hill Cove before summer is out. Between that, kids going back to school, and everything else you have going on, I know it's busy. I just wanted to catch you for a second, because **the Democratic primary for governor is only 17 days away.**
**[As the primary approaches, can you click here and give $27 or as much as you can?](https://organizing.ginaraimondo.com/page/m/1f41a0e5/5e076a9e/54153232/14425ae8/2685870963/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raimondo?refcode=180826")**
**I 've been thinking about how far we've come in the last four years.** We have created thousands of jobs -- Rhode Island has more jobs today than ever before. We've cut taxes, cut red tape and cut regulations to make it easier for small businesses to thrive. And we're making sure we have workers ready to take these jobs. Our unemployment has dropped one-third since I took office. We're number one in public pre-K quality. We have all-day kindergarten and computer science classes in every public school. Rhode Island now offers free PSATs and SATs and we're making a $1 billion investment in fixing schools.
**In spite of all of this, what keeps me up at night is how much more work there is to do.** Too many Rhode Islanders are still being left behind. We must continue this momentum to ensure our comeback reaches every corner of the state.
**We couldn 't have made it this far without your contribution, and in order to finish the job I need your support once more.**
**[Click here to give $27 or as much as you can right now to get this campaign over the finish line.](https://organizing.ginaraimondo.com/page/m/1f41a0e5/5e076a9e/54153232/14425ae8/2685870963/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raimondo?refcode=180826")**
Many thanks,
Gina

name: =?utf-8?Q?Hayden=20Shamel=20for=20Arkansas?=
subject: Take Action: Women's Equality Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HAYDEN SHAMEL FOR ARKANSAS
body:
| | | |
---
| | | Friends: Today, on Women's Equality Day, we commemorate the ratification of the 19th Amendment in August 1920 that granted women the right to vote, and we give thanks to our foremothers who fought valiantly to make that right a reality. Today, we celebrate our successes even as we recognize how far we still have to go. Very few women realize that they are not actually guaranteed equal rights under the Constitution of the United States; the Equal Rights Amendment, while passed by Congress in 1972, failed to pass the necessary ratification by three-fourths of the states. Arkansas was among the states that did not vote to ratify. Celebrating equality for women, or people of color, or our LGBTQ community, or any other historically marginalized group is not an attack on those who do not belong. It is an affirmation of our worth and a reminder that the struggle for human rights for all people is one that we must recommit ourselves to every single day. So today, I give thanks for the right of women across this country to participate in free and fair elections. Today, I thank the suffragettes who committed their lives to ensuring that women have a voice in the American political process. And today, I turn to take the reigns from our brave foremothers to continue their work. We will not give up. We will not give in. We will continue the fight.
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | Friends: Today, on Women's Equality Day, we commemorate the ratification of the 19th Amendment in August 1920 that granted women the right to vote, and we give thanks to our foremothers who fought valiantly to make that right a reality. Today, we celebrate our successes even as we recognize how far we still have to go. Very few women realize that they are not actually guaranteed equal rights under the Constitution of the United States; the Equal Rights Amendment, while passed by Congress in 1972, failed to pass the necessary ratification by three-fourths of the states. Arkansas was among the states that did not vote to ratify. Celebrating equality for women, or people of color, or our LGBTQ community, or any other historically marginalized group is not an attack on those who do not belong. It is an affirmation of our worth and a reminder that the struggle for human rights for all people is one that we must recommit ourselves to every single day. So today, I give thanks for the right of women across this country to participate in free and fair elections. Today, I thank the suffragettes who committed their lives to ensuring that women have a voice in the American political process. And today, I turn to take the reigns from our brave foremothers to continue their work. We will not give up. We will not give in. We will continue the fight. | | | JOIN THE FIGHT! name: Jessica Morse
subject: Senator McCain: the legacy we must live
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jessica Morse for Congress, a grassroots campaign
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/V2VLeSzTGzvVWrX6WzEHPIhPze8AvnJ74v1Gz8Z-890=/na02-rgcJRacmraLWw-X4HMyin9TVz3SLXCxnXGcc6I=?recurring=auto&refcode=email_ngp_fr_20180826_JM_20180826-senjm-all_all_logo)
Friends,
I awoke this morning feeling the weight of Senator McCain's passing. He set the standard for public service and patriotism that I strive to have the courage and character to emulate.
Courage. Patriotism. Principle. Senator McCain embodied the ideals of our democracy and he fought hard to defend them. He stood up for what he believed was right, not what was easy. He admitted when he made a mistake and defended the humanity of his political adversaries. Senator McCain was a member of the old guard of true leaders who set the standard of civility and independent thinking that I strive to follow.
This is a moment in our history when we need leaders to bridge partisan divisions and steadfastly place the sanctity of our democracy and the sovereignty of our nation first. It's tempting to be discouraged, but it is up to us to take up the mantle. We must strive every day to defend our democracy by seeing each other without partisan lenses. We must be unfailing in our appetite for policy nuance and deeply skeptical of rhetoric intended to elicit a purely visceral reaction. We must think in terms of generations, not political cycles.
We must think as people, not as a party. I cringe when I hear some say, "THE Democrats" or "THE Republicans"--let's drop these barriers. As Senator McCain said, we have a "big, boisterous, brawling, intemperate, striving, daring, beautiful, bountiful, brave, magnificent country." We have to re-learn the art of dialogue--listening with respect to someone with a different perspective. When empathy disappears, when understanding erodes, our vibrant democracy fades.
I will strive to hold myself to McCain's standard--to do what is right, not what is politically easy; to listen to others; to fight for long-term solutions and attempt to clarify any mistakes with good humor and grace. As a public servant and as an American, I hope we can all strive to follow his enormous example and defend the democracy that he lived and embodied so beautifully.
I send my deepest sympathies to the McCain family and all those who called him a friend and mentor.
Thank you,
Jessica

name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: Senator McCain
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Klobuchar for Minnesota
body:
Hi,
I was deeply saddened to hear of the passing of my friend and colleague, Senator John McCain. In the Navy, he exemplified heroism and bravery. In the Senate, he was a mentor to so many senators: he taught us how to work with leaders on the world stage, and he taught us how to work with each other regardless of party back at home.
This past month my husband and I got to visit John and Cindy at their ranch in Arizona. While battling brain cancer, he continued to be engaged in the issues of our time, always a man of grit. My last memory of John was something that happened on that visit. He pointed to a sentence in his new book and told me "that's all that matters." The sentence was this: **"Nothing in life is more liberating than to fight for a cause larger than yourself."**
That's what Senator McCain's entire life was about. He taught us that as a POW in Vietnam when he made the decision to allow others to be released before him so he didn't get special treatment. Supporting his fellow soldiers was a cause greater than himself.
He showed it as a senator when he voted to maintain health care and stood up for immigrants. Those were causes greater than himself and party politics.
He showed it as a candidate for President when he publicly disagreed with a woman in my state at a rally who questioned the patriotism of his Democratic opponent Barack Obama. He defended his opponent. The dignity of America was a cause greater than himself.
Finally he loved his family so very much.
He has always been surrounded by strong women--his wife and my friend Cindy, his mom, and his daughters. I know that they--along with the rest of his family--are all grieving, as is our entire country. Our country owes a lot to John McCain and we will greatly miss him.
\--Amy

"" | | "" | "" | "" \------FORWARDED MESSAGE------ FROM: Anne Holton SUBJECT: I'm so proud of Tim Derek, I am so proud of the work Tim has done. "" | "" | "" | "" | "" Between now and Election Day, the campaign will be getting the word out in every way we can.
